query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "when vision speaks for sound" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on medical image registration (aligning histology and MRI) using contrastive learning, which is a distinct domain and mechanism from the user's question regarding "When Vision Speaks for Sound" (a cross-modal audio-visual LLM framework). There is no overlap in the empirical population, the specific cross-modal mechanism (sound-to-vision vs. vision-to-sound), or the canonical vocabulary clusters for either task.
